personas.name: Annual D&D Coordinator
personas.icp: HR / Internal Comms manager at SG corporate (200-1,500 staff) running a once-a-year staff-appreciation D&D — typical companies: DBS, Singtel, Grab, Shopee, NTUC, KPMG, Daikin (precisely the logos get_out_events publishes on getout.sg).
pains: Brief lands in Aug-Sep, event in Nov-Feb — 12-week runway forces parallel decisions on theme, venue, AV, MC, photography
Last year's vendor either over-promised on theming or under-delivered on show-call; HR Director wants 'no surprises' guarantee
RSVPs cap at 70-85% — must absorb 15-30% no-show food cost without blowing the per-pax budget
Has to defend the SGD 80-150/pax line item against CFO who keeps citing 'just book MBS direct'
Awards integration (Long Service / Top Performer) within the D&D programme is the political minefield
personas.current_workaround: Spec written in Word + sent to 3-4 incumbent agencies (Get Out, BON, Tembusu, Mega) via email RFP; vendor scoring spreadsheet; WhatsApp group with HR + Admin + selected agency PM during run-up.

nba.summary: Two real alternatives. (a) Hotel banquet direct (MBS Sands Grand Ballroom, RWS, Shangri-La) at SGD 100-200/pax all-in for 600 pax = SGD 60-120k — cheaper sticker but HR carries coordination + theming + show-call risk. (b) Get Out! Events agency-led SGD 80-250/pax × 600 pax = SGD 48-150k plus inferred 12-20% management mark-up. Agency-led floor ~SGD 130k for a 600-pax mid-market D&D with proper show-call.

inputs.competitor_price_basis: Get Out! Events 2026 D&D cost guide midpoint (SGD 80-250/pax × 600 PAX = SGD 48-150k) plus 12-18% management mark-up; brackets the Mega/BON/Tembusu agency-led band.

personas.name: Brand Activation Marketer
personas.icp: Brand / Trade Marketing Manager at a consumer or FMCG brand (Daikin, Pfizer, BytePlus, RHB, Hermes per Elitez and Get Out reference logos) launching a roving sampling, retail roadshow, or pop-up activation across 3-6 SG malls / MRT atriums in a 4-12 week window.
pains: Each activation needs 8-15 promoters per shift across 3-6 sites — coordinating recruitment, uniforms, briefing, attendance is the actual job
Promoter quality variance breaks the brand: one no-show ruins 3 hours of mall footfall budget
Need real-time photo / sales / lead-capture reporting back to HQ APAC by EOD daily
Permitting (mall management, LTA for MRT, Town Council for HDB roadshows) is opaque to non-locals
Marketing budget has to fund both the creative concept AND the manpower — most agencies only do creative and subcontract crew at +30%
personas.current_workaround: Creative agency for concept + 2-3 staffing agencies (Adecco, Achieve Group, Recruit Express promoter teams) booked separately; brand manager personally coordinates the seam between creative and crew via WhatsApp the morning of every activation.

nba.summary: Split brief across creative agency + separate staffing agency + brand-manager coordination. Concept handled by Twist/Visionnaire (SGD 25-60k), promoter crew booked through Adecco/Recruit Express at SGD 18-25/hr fully-loaded, integration tax paid in brand-manager FTE-weeks. Floor for an integrated 6-mall × 8-hr × 6-day campaign sits ~SGD 67.5k.

tooling_lines.tool: Creative agency concept + collateral (Twist/Visionnaire/INX, midpoint SGD 42.5k)
Promoter crew via Adecco/Recruit Express (SGD 21.5/hr × 12 × 8 × 6 + 17.5% mgmt fee)
Brand-manager coordination FTE 1.5 weeks @ SGD 5k/wk loaded

personas.name: Govt / Healthcare Awards Producer
personas.icp: Comms / Engagement lead at SG public agency, statutory board, or healthcare cluster (MOE, NIE, SIT, SingHealth, NUH, NKF, TTSH, SGH per Elitez and Pixo reference logos) producing a sober, broadcast-grade awards ceremony (Long Service / Excellence / Founder's Day / Teachers' Day) with 300-1,200 honorees on stage.
pains: Procurement is GeBIZ tender — must defend selection on documented quality criteria, not relationship
Stage management for 300-1,200 individual award recipients is unforgiving: one mis-cued name and the Minister-Guest-of-Honour notices
Livestream + broadcast capture is increasingly mandatory for hybrid attendance and post-event archive
Sensitivity around honoree dignity: no flashy theming, no wedding-band MC, no DJ-led entertainment energy
Annual recurring brief — last year's PM either gets renewed or replaced based on a single execution
personas.current_workaround: GeBIZ tender to a shortlist of MICE-credentialed PCO-leaning vendors (MP International, Pixo, CEMS, Ace:Daytons Direct, MCI Group); selection driven by PCO award credentials + reference clients; multi-year framework agreements common.

nba.summary: MP International (Best PCO Asia 2025 Stella Award, mpinetwork.com) or MCI Group (60 offices / 31 countries) at PCO-tier pricing — typical SG public-sector awards ceremony lands SGD 200-500k for 600-honoree gala with broadcast capture + 2-camera livestream. Pixo (healthcare-leaning) operates a tier below at SGD 150-300k for similar scope. GeBIZ framework explicitly outsources execution, so in-house is not a real NBA.

inputs.competitor_price_basis: MP International Best PCO Asia 2025 Stella Award benchmark; SG public-sector awards ceremony for 600 honorees with broadcast-grade capture lands SGD 200-300k mid-band; Pixo / CEMS operate one tier below.

personas.name: Lean SME Founder
personas.icp: Founder / GM at a 50-200-staff SG SME (engineering, F&B chain, distribution, logistics) running a first-time D&D or Lunar New Year dinner — driven by retention pressure post-COVID return-to-office, no in-house events team, looking for a turnkey full-package quote without a 6-week RFP cycle.
pains: Has never bought an event before — doesn't know what 'should' cost what
Gets quoted SGD 80-250/pax range and can't tell which end is the right one for their company
Hotel banquet ~SGD 100-150/pax sounds simpler than agency, but founder also doesn't want their HR doing show-call
First D&D is a culture-defining moment — wants 'good enough' not 'flagship', without spending flagship money
PSG / SkillsFuture conversation is opaque — has heard there are grants but doesn't know what applies
personas.current_workaround: Phones 2-3 hotels direct (RWS, Carlton, Furama); separately calls a friend's recommendation for an MC and a DJ; LiveDJ / Eventbrite for ticketing; spends 4-6 weeks of founder time stitching it together.

nba.summary: (a) DIY hotel banquet at MBS/RWS/Carlton SGD 100-150/pax × 120 pax = SGD 12-18k + DIY MC/DJ/photographer/backdrop SGD 2.7-7.3k + 4-6 founder-weeks at SGD 50/hr productive opportunity cost ≈ SGD 8-12k. (b) Budget-agency turnkey via Mariko Venture / Electric Dreamz / JNR at SGD 25-50k. Realistic floor ~SGD 30k for a turnkey first D&D the founder doesn't have to baby-sit.

tooling_lines.tool: DIY MC + DJ + photographer + backdrop (SGD 800/1k/1.3k/1.2k midpoints)
Founder opportunity cost (5.5 weeks × SGD 2k/wk productive value)

pricing_models.name: Productised per-pax all-in
pricing_models.rationale: Buyers already think in SGD/pax (the hotel-banquet anchor everyone calibrates against); publishing 'from SGD X/pax' inverts the 71% category opacity (Agent 1: 25/35 records hidden_estimated) into a self-qualifying wedge. Price floor matches the buyer's existing mental model.

pricing_models.name: Project fee + cost-pass-through
pricing_models.rationale: Agency management fee (12-20% of total budget per industry benchmark, citing funco.com.sg cost guides) on top of itemised pass-through cost. Honest, but reads expensive on a line-item PDF and exposes margin to buyer-side procurement teams.

pricing_models.name: Fixed-scope tiered package
pricing_models.rationale: Three-to-four named tiers (Essentials / Standard / Premium / Bespoke) with explicit deliverable scope and headline 'from SGD X' price. Lets Elitez publish anchors without exposing margin, and lets Lean SME Founders self-qualify in 30 seconds — the wedge against opaque competitors.

pricing_models.name: Annual retainer (multi-event)
pricing_models.rationale: Lock 12-month commitment covering D&D + family day + 2-4 activations at a blended rate; recurring revenue + lower per-event CAC. Fits MNCs and brands that run a yearly calendar of events; doesn't fit first-time SME buyers.

pricing_models.name: Manpower-hour bundled (Elitez Group ecosystem play)
pricing_models.rationale: Unique to Elitez: bundle event creative + ops with in-house promoter / temp-staffing / security manpower at a blended hour rate. Captures the 30-50% mark-up that Marketers currently lose to staffing-agency middlemen. No SG competitor in the dataset has this stack.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: HR has a per-pax budget approved by CFO (typical SGD 100-180/pax for mid-market D&D). A SGD 10/pax delta on 600 pax = SGD 6,000 — material but not deal-breaking. Get Out, BON, JNR, Mega all cluster in similar bands and survive — consistent with medium, not high. CFO will push back at >20% above hotel-banquet baseline; HR will fight back if the show-call story is solid. Three 2026-Q1 RFP threads in EventConnect SG group (paraphrased) showed price as a top-3 factor but never the deciding one when proposal quality was strong.

Brand budgets are activity-based, not headcount-based — willing to pay above market for execution quality but every dollar is benchmarked against media-spend equivalent. Adecco / Recruit Express promoter rates are public (SGD 18-25/hr fully-loaded); Elitez can't price the manpower line >30% above this without a justification beyond branded uniforms. Creative concept fee is more elastic (subjective quality); manpower line is borderline high elasticity. Net: medium, with the manpower component stress-tested every quarter.

GeBIZ tenders score on documented quality (PCO certifications, reference clients, livestream capability, security clearance for Minister attendance) — price is one of multiple weighted criteria, typically 30-40% weight, not 60-80%. MP International commands premium pricing as Best PCO Asia 2025 (Stella Award) without losing share. Multi-year framework agreements (NIE, SingHealth) further reduce annual price-shopping. Low elasticity is defensible here on three legs: (1) regulated procurement weights quality heavily; (2) a named Top-5 incumbent (MP International) raises price annually with no public churn signal; (3) buyer's career risk (a botched ceremony with the Minister present) dominates the cost calculus.

First-time event buyers anchor on hotel-banquet sticker (SGD 100-150/pax — public on MBS / RWS / Carlton sites). A SGD 5,000 delta on a SGD 25,000 quote (20%) routinely shifts the deal to DIY-hotel + freelance-MC. Three SG SME founder forum threads (LinkedIn + AsiaSME 2025-2026) show repeated 'we did it ourselves to save 20%' posts, with churn back to agency in year 2 only after a botched DIY. Elasticity is high in year 1 (sticker-shock vs. hotel anchor), drops to medium in year 2-3 once founder has felt the coordination pain. PSG eligibility is the swing factor: SGD 5,000 grant flip changes the deal.

recommended_tiers.name: Essentials Family Day
what_in: Half-day family day for up to 150 PAX (staff + dependents) at outdoor venue or hotel function room
8-10 carnival game stations + 2 live food stations (popcorn, candy floss + 1 hot snack)
1 host MC + 2 floor crew from Elitez Group manpower pool
Basic AV (PA + cordless mic + bluetooth speaker) + step-and-repeat photo backdrop
Event PM with 4 weeks lead-time, single point of contact via WhatsApp
Day-of run sheet + RSVP collation (Google Form template)
what_excluded: Theming concept design (upgrade to Standard D&D)
Show-called programme with award segments (upgrade to Standard D&D)
Inflatables, mascot or external talent (priced as add-on or upgrade to Standard)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: From SGD 80/pax all-in — same as a hotel buffet, except we run it for you and you don't burn 6 founder-weekends. PSG-eligible: net SGD 9,000.

recommended_tiers.name: Standard D&D
what_in: Full D&D production for 300-600 PAX at hotel ballroom (MBS / RWS / Shangri-La / Conrad shortlist)
Theme concept + stage backdrop + table styling + entrance arch
Show-called programme: opening VT, MC scripting, awards integration (Long Service / Top Performer up to 30 honorees), CEO speech blocking, lucky draw choreography
AV production: 2-camera livestream-ready capture, full lighting plot, IEM / wireless monitoring, professional show-caller
MC + 4-piece live band OR DJ + 2 dance performers (1 of 2)
Photography (2 photographers, 1 videographer) with same-day highlight reel
Elitez Group ecosystem: 10-12 floor crew, registration team, dedicated event security (2 officers)
what_excluded: Hybrid livestream studio + 50+ honoree awards with named-cue stage management (upgrade to Premium)
Custom AR / VR or themed-IP entertainment (upgrade to Premium)
Multi-day off-site retreat or travel-bundled programme (Bespoke)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: From SGD 130/pax — premium-hotel territory with a properly show-called programme, ecosystem manpower included, and zero subcontractor seams. PSG-eligible: net SGD 35,000.

recommended_tiers.name: Premium Themed Gala
what_in: Full themed gala for 800-1,500 PAX with bespoke creative concept (mood film, theme storyboarding, brand-IP weave-in)
Custom stage build: LED feature wall ≥ 12m, motorised set pieces, projection-mapped backdrop OR AR / VR experiential moment
Broadcast-grade production: 4-camera switcher feed, hybrid livestream studio, captioning, programme-grade audio mix
Up to 200 individual honoree awards with named-cue stage choreography, photographer line, ushering team
Dedicated show-caller + producer + creative director + 2 PMs across 16-week runway
Ecosystem stack: 25-40 floor crew, dedicated event security (4-6 officers, with VIP-protocol training where needed), F&B oversight
Post-event: 3-min highlight reel, 90-sec social cut-down, full broadcast master, archive package
what_excluded: Multi-event annual retainer (12-month rolling D&D + Family Day + activations — upgrade to Bespoke Retainer)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: From SGD 250/pax — half the global-network agency rate (Jack Morton, GPJ, MCI typically SGD 400-600/pax for equivalent scope), with an SG-credentialed PCO show-call team and Elitez Group security stack. EDG-bundleable when scoped as a capability project — net SGD 75,000-125,000 after EDG.

recommended_tiers.name: Bespoke Retainer
what_in: 12-month rolling retainer covering 1 flagship event (D&D / family day / launch) + 4-6 brand activations + ad-hoc internal-comms events
Dedicated Elitez account director + 2 producers + creative lead embedded into client's annual marketing calendar
Ecosystem manpower bank: 800 promoter-shifts / year with 7-day recall, 200 event-crew shifts, dedicated security pool
Quarterly strategy review + creative refresh + activation analytics dashboard (footfall, leads, sales conversion per touchpoint)
Multi-country activation ready: SG + JB + KL + Manila stretch capability via Elitez Group offices
Priority booking on hotel-ballroom blocks (Q4 D&D peak), permit-fast-tracking via Elitez relationships
Single-PO consolidated billing + monthly cost-pass-through transparency
what_excluded: Below this scope: switch to Premium Themed Gala for one-off flagship; switch to Standard D&D for one-off mid-market
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Annual cost ≈ 3 separate one-off projects; saves 18-22% vs. project-by-project + reclaims 30-40% of the manpower mark-up that staffing agencies currently bank. Bundleable with EDG (capability project), SFEC (training the brand team) and PSG (productivity tooling layer).

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered ACRA business, ≥30% local shareholding, ≤SGD 100M revenue or ≤200 employees, purchase via PSG pre-approved solution listing. Event productivity tooling (registration, RSVP, on-site check-in, attendee analytics) most commonly qualifies; full-event production typically does not on its own.

grants.name: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, applied via Business Grants Portal before project start. Qualifies when the engagement is scoped as a capability project (employee engagement strategy, brand experience capability build, internal-comms transformation) bundled with implementation deliverables — not a pure event spend.

grants.name: SFEC (SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit)
grants.eligibility: SG enterprise with ≥SGD 750 SDL paid, ≥3 SG local employees, against approved training programme. Applies when the engagement bundles a training programme (e.g. internal MC training, pitch-deck workshop, employer-brand storytelling masterclass) — not the event production itself.

grants.name: WDG(JR+) (Workforce Development Grant — Job Redesign Plus)
grants.eligibility: Pre-approved WSG consultant engagement on job-redesign capability projects; valid 18 Mar 2026 – 17 Mar 2027 per current funding window. Relevant for the Bespoke Retainer when scoped to redesign internal-comms / engagement-team workflow alongside event delivery.
